June 2025: Make Health Tech Great Again

The White House Created a New Healthcare Technology Ecosystem. It Needs Infrastructure.

President Trump, Secretary Kennedy, and Dr. Oz are breaking down barriers to create a patient-centered ecosystem and introducing new outcome-based payment models. All of it requires technology infrastructure.

CMS
Health IT Ecosystem
Breaking Down Barriers
The old system was silos and information blocking. CMS created a new Health IT Ecosystem — 60+ companies pledged.
10-Year
ACCESS Model
New Payment Models
Outcome-based payments that reward results, not volume. ACCESS Model launches July 5, 2026.
141M
Beneficiaries
Patient Empowerment
Full interoperability by July 4, 2026. Patients own records. New Medicare app store gives patients real tools.
300K+
Physician Groups
Massive Provider Market
Most lack the technology to participate in outcome-based models. They need infrastructure. That\u2019s what we sell.

This administration is building the rails. InPursuit is the train.

Two Doors Opened. We Walk Through Both.

Approved for All 3 Patient-Facing Apps in the New CMS Medicare App Store.

That single approval opens two massive doors simultaneously. Most companies can only serve one side. InPursuit was purpose-built to serve both — with the same infrastructure.

Door One

141 Million Patients

Harness the Power of Your Data™

Every Medicare and Medicaid beneficiary gets access through the new CMS app store. Unified health record, AI intelligence, virtual care, wearable integration. Free tier mandated by CMS. Premium tiers unlock advanced capabilities.

Door Two

300,000+ Physician Groups

Results Matter™

The same TETRA™ infrastructure powers outcome-based payment models for providers. CMS ACCESS Model launches July 5. InPursuit provides the technology at zero upfront cost and takes a percentage of the new revenue generated.

One infrastructure. Two massive markets. No one else can serve both.

Door One: Direct to 141 Million Americans

MVP Due March 31. White House Launch Event July 4. We’re In It.

CMS is building a vetted app store on Medicare.gov. InPursuit is one of a handful delivering all three patient-facing app categories. Our MVP is due March 31. On July 4th, the White House will showcase technology companies at a national launch event. We will be there.

MVP Deliverable
3 patient-facing apps ready

JUL 4

Three CMS App Categories — All Three Approved

Chronic Disease Management
Diabetes, obesity, hypertension, depression. AI monitoring, care gaps, alerts.
Conversational AI
Patient-facing AI for symptom checking, care navigation, scheduling.
Digital Check-In
Replace paper intake. QR codes, Smart Health Cards, digital identity.

Access to 141 million Americans. Day one. Through the Medicare app store.

Door Two: A New Outcome-Based Payment Model

CMS Pays Providers for Results. InPursuit Provides the Infrastructure. We Share the Revenue.

How InPursuit Earns
CMS pays provider per patient/month~$47
× 4,000 eligible patients (avg group)$2.3M/yr
InPursuit provides the tech (zero upfront)
InPursuit revenue share25–35%
InPursuit earns per group$575K–$805K/yr

Zero upfront cost. Revenue share only.

At Scale
10 Providers$5.7M–$8M/yr
50 Providers$28M–$40M/yr
100 Providers$57M–$80M/yr
First Signed Partner
Honeycomb Medical Group
Memphis, TN  |  22,000 patients  |  ~6,400 Medicare
Deployed inside eClinicalWorks
Estimated InPursuit revenue: ~$805K/yr

10-year model. Rolling admissions through 2033. The longest runway CMS has ever offered.

$2M Bridges Us to Revenue. $20M Captures Both Markets.
